current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.
architecture_findings: round 1: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

failure_patterns.pattern: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations.

gated.design_hypothesis: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations.
next_steps: If gated beats TopK on proxy FVU, expose and sweep gated_temperature to sharpen or soften support selection.
If gated shows quality promise but poor throughput, follow with a performance pass rather than discarding the family.
If gated underperforms badly at default settings, try a stabilization round on family-specific bias/temperature before abandoning the family.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations.

recent_insights: Round 1: variable isolation warning: Multiple primary variables changed simultaneously: code_edit, architecture
Added a new 'gated' architecture family under sparsify/ with a separate gate encoder and default-compatible config fields.
Validated basic forward/backward and save/load for the new gated SAE locally before proposing the run.
Round stays focused on architecture exploration; K remains at the existing anchor and no lower-K claim is being made.
